---
title: "URL Extract"
author: "Chelsea Bihlmeyer"
date: "2026-02-08"
output: html_document
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# --- Read Excel, create permalink column, save XLSX ---

# Set  file paths 
input_xlsx <- "meta_sans_trans.xlsx"
output_xlsx <- "id_url_for_trans.xlsx"    

# 1) Load the Excel-reading package
# install.packages("readxl")
library(readxl)

# 2) Read the Excel file (defaults to the first sheet)
df <- read_excel(input_xlsx)

# 3) Treat IDs/handles as text (prevents scientific notation or trimming issues)
df$item_id <- as.character(df$item_id)
df$author_handle <- trimws(as.character(df$author_handle))

# 4) Build the TikTok permalink in a new column called 'tiktokurl'
#    Format: https://www.tiktok.com/@{handle}/video/{item_id}
df$tiktokurl <- paste0("https://www.tiktok.com/@", df$author_handle, "/video/", df$item_id)

# 5) Save to XLSX (no row numbers; empty strings for NAs)
writexl::write_xlsx(df, output_xlsx)

# (Optional) Peek at the first few results to verify
# head(df[, c("item_id", "author_handle", "tiktokurl")], 5)
```
